evidence: We're not here to replace your product. We're the missing layer that makes your product defensible in the long run.
Careerspan isn't a replacement for Handshake—it's the missing layer that makes their products defensible. Distribution platforms have solved access, but access without signal is noise.

Handshake has 1,600 universities but no way to capture behavioral signal at scale. Careerspan has the coaching infrastructure to capture signal but lacks distribution. The combination creates a data moat neither could build alone.
Real angle is data supremacy. Instead of trying to sell Careerspan as a product, you're selling the opportunity to build a proprietary dataset that competitors can't replicate.

Positioning Careerspan as a strategic hedge for Handshake's declining core business ($200M, only 6% growth). Even if the partnership never becomes a major revenue line, it provides data supremacy and option value.

Career span has built the signal technology only platform that can tell employers who to hire, not just whom to reach. Access without signal is noise. Employers have 20 million profiles with no way to identify who will succeed.
We're a three-person team. Like we're not even that expensive essentially to bring on. Emphasizing the 3-person team as an asset (low acquisition overhead) turns a potential weakness into strength.
The real value isn't in helping someone get one job—it's in tracking their entire career trajectory and predicting future success. This creates a dataset that compounds in value over time.
